mission.title: Install and Configure Grpc on Kubernetes
mission.description: gRPC is a modern, open-source high-performance remote procedure call (RPC) framework that enables client and server applications to communicate transparently. Installing gRPC allows you to build connected systems efficiently across various programming languages.

steps.title: Add the gRPC Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official gRPC Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration to access the gRPC installation chart.
```bash
helm repo add grpc https://charts.grpc.io
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install gRPC using Helm
steps.description:
Install gRPC in your Kubernetes cluster using Helm, specifying the version and creating a namespace if it doesn't exist.
```bash
helm install grpc grpc/grpc --version 1.78.1 --namespace grpc --create-namespace
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the gRPC pods are running successfully in the cluster.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace grpc
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits
steps.description:
Set resource limits for the gRPC deployment to ensure it runs efficiently in your cluster.
```bash
kubectl patch deployment grpc --namespace grpc -p '{"spec":{"template":{"spec":{"containers":[{"name":"grpc","resources":{"limits":{"cpu":"500m","memory":"512Mi"}}}]}}}}' 
```

steps.title: Set up port forwarding for local access
steps.description:
Forward a local port to the gRPC service to access it from your local machine.
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/grpc --namespace grpc 50051:50051
```

resolution.summary: After completing these steps, you should have a running instance of gRPC in your Kubernetes cluster, with the pods successfully running and accessible via port forwarding. You can now start building your applications using gRPC.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add grpc https://charts.grpc.io
helm repo update

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the gRPC release from your Kubernetes cluster using Helm to remove all associated resources except for CRDs and persistent volumes.
```bash
helm uninstall grpc --namespace grpc
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and persistent resources
uninstall.description:
Remove any Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) and persistent volume claims (PVCs) that were created during the installation.
```bash
kubectl delete crd grpc.*
kubectl delete pvc --all --namespace grpc
```

uninstall.title: Remove namespace and verify
uninstall.description:
Delete the gRPC namespace and verify that all resources have been cleaned up.
```bash
kubectl delete namespace grpc
kubectl get all --namespace grpc
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up the current state of the gRPC deployment to ensure you can restore it if needed.
```bash
helm get values grpc --namespace grpc > grpc-backup-values.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update repo and run upgrade command
upgrade.description:
Update the Helm repository and upgrade the gRPC release to the latest version. Replace '1.79.0' with the desired version.
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade grpc grpc/grpc --version 1.79.0 --namespace grpc
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check that the gRPC pods are running the new version successfully after the upgrade.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace grpc
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
If the gRPC pods are stuck in a CrashLoopBackOff state, check the logs for errors that might indicate what is wrong.
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace grpc
```

troubleshooting.title: Service not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
If you cannot access the gRPC service, ensure that the port forwarding is set up correctly and that the service is running.
```bash
kubectl get svc --namespace grpc
kubectl port-forward svc/grpc --namespace grpc 50051:50051
```

troubleshooting.title: Insufficient resources
troubleshooting.description:
If the deployment fails due to insufficient resources, check the resource limits and the available resources in your cluster.
```bash
kubectl describe deployment grpc --namespace grpc
kubectl get nodes -o=jsonpath='{.items[*].status.allocatable}'
```

troubleshooting.title: Failed to connect to the gRPC server
troubleshooting.description:
If clients are unable to connect to the gRPC server, verify the network policies and firewall rules that may be blocking traffic.
```bash
kubectl get networkpolicy --namespace grpc
```

prerequisites.description: You need a Kubernetes cluster and the Helm and kubectl command-line tools installed on your local machine.
